description: Baseline LazyLoader CHAINLOAD plus built-in splash skip full-oracle fast-load run

asi.hypothesis: establish fresh baseline for the new fast-load target using the already-supported LazyLoader CHAINLOAD plus built-in splash skip path
asi.learned: Baseline full-oracle fast_load_seconds is 45.449s with 1400/1400, zero simulated input, save_safety_ok=1, 22 world-stable samples, and 27/27 save identity matches. This is the bar for future fast-load changes.
asi.next_action_hint: use static RE around external failed RVA 0xb0c3ed vs built-in working RVA 0xb0c35d to identify the missing fast-load phase before adding any new patch

description: Product autoload now auto-enables built-in splash skip with no explicit env/file

asi.hypothesis: the external skipper's useful fast-load piece is the BeginLogo branch flip already implemented internally; making product_autoload_enabled() arm splash_skip_enabled() turns it into a core default with no env/file dependency
asi.learned: With no ER_EFFECTS_SPLASH_SKIP env/file in the runtime request, product autoload patched splash-skip internally and still hit 1400/1400 at 44.479s, zero simulated input, save_safety_ok=1, and 27/27 save identity matches.
asi.next_action_hint: validate the static mapping: external broken RVA 0xb0c3ed is old-version equivalent of current working branch at 0xb0c35d (offset delta -0x90); if confirmed, document and avoid adding another patch.

description: Confirmed core auto-splash product autoload improves full-oracle fast-load time

asi.hypothesis: rerunning the same no-env auto-splash candidate should confirm the improvement is not a contaminated one-off
asi.learned: Second no-env auto-splash run hit 42.085s with 1400/1400, zero simulated input, save_safety_ok=1, and 27/27 identity matches. Evidence reinforces that product_autoload_enabled() should own splash_skip_enabled() by default.
asi.next_action_hint: continue with static-only investigation of the external DLL target before adding more patches; do not add a second splash patch unless static evidence shows another distinct startup phase.

description: Reduce live-dialog activation settle to 60 frames while preserving full oracle

asi.hypothesis: the previous 90-frame live-dialog activation settle was conservative; 60 frames should still let the native ProfileLoadDialog register before slot activation while shaving startup latency
asi.learned: 60-frame settle preserved full 1400/1400 correctness and improved fast_load_seconds to 40.716. This suggests the live dialog is ready well before the old 90-frame wait in the current release path.
asi.next_action_hint: try a smaller settle such as 30 frames only if it remains self-validating; the full oracle and save identity must stay green.

description: 30-frame live-dialog settle is too aggressive and produced invalid/no-load evidence

asi.hypothesis: 30-frame live-dialog activation settle may still be enough to mount the selected save faster
asi.rollback_reason: full oracle failed: fast_load_seconds=999, north_star_score=0, autoload_success=0, player_available=0, selected_slot_loaded=0, false_positives=1; runtime also hit the run_experiment wall despite driver cleanup
asi.next_action_hint: do not reduce live-dialog activation settle below 60 without adding a new readiness signal; 30 frames can yield misleading visual/world-stable evidence without selected save/player correctness
asi.evidence: 30-frame run left c30=0xa010000 and name_len=0 while visual_llm_world_expected=1, proving the full oracle is necessary and this is not a safe fast-load shortcut

description: 45-frame live-dialog settle rerun looked visually loaded but failed structured oracle

asi.hypothesis: 45-frame settle might be a safe middle ground between successful 60 and failed 30 frames.
asi.rollback_reason: User-observed rerun proved the visual state was misleading: screenshot showed Checking save data / title-style screen and structured oracle had autoload_success=0, selected_slot_loaded=0, oracle_player_present=0, false_positives=1.
asi.evidence: /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/smoke/fast-load-product-autosplash-20260619/inworld.png showed Checking save data; metrics from /tmp/fast-load-45-rerun-watch.out: fast_load_seconds=999, north_star_score=0, player_available=1, selected_slot_loaded=0, oracle_player_present=0, false_positives=1.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep 60-frame settle as the safe fixed threshold. Do not use visual/LLM world signal alone for fast-load decisions; require selected save/player oracle.

description: 15-frame initial own-stepper settle reaches structured world faster but misses visual full-oracle score

asi.hypothesis: initial title-state settle before forcing BeginLogo can be reduced from 30 to 15 frames to start the proven menu/load path earlier
asi.rollback_reason: primary metric failed because strict full-oracle score requires visual_llm_world_expected=1; run reached structured world at 35.276s with 1325 score but not the full 1400 release proof
asi.next_action_hint: inspect /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/smoke/fast-load-settle15-20260619/inworld.png; if it is a visual false negative with structured world proof, investigate screenshot/cleanup timing rather than blindly keeping 15-frame settle
asi.learned: 15-frame initial settle is a promising speed path: selected save loaded, player/world stable, zero simulated input, 27/27 save identity, 35.276s time_to_player; only visual_llm_world_expected failed.

description: Reduce initial own-stepper title settle to 25 frames with full oracle intact

asi.hypothesis: 25-frame initial title settle may avoid the unsafe 15-frame modal race while still starting the proven BeginLogo/menu path earlier than the 30-frame default
asi.learned: 25-frame initial settle preserved the full 1400/1400 oracle, visible world, zero simulated input, save_safety_ok=1, 27/27 save identity matches, and improved fast_load_seconds to 33.477.
asi.next_action_hint: confirm 25-frame settle with a repeat run before considering it stable; do not try 15 again without a new readiness predicate because it produced no visible character/modal return-to-menu risk.

description: 25-frame initial settle confirmation full-oracle rerun was slower than prior kept sample

asi.hypothesis: repeat the 25-frame initial title settle candidate to confirm the full-oracle success was not a contaminated one-off
asi.learned: 25-frame settle repeated with full 1400/1400 oracle, zero simulated input, save_safety_ok=1, and visible player/world, but measured 34.724s versus the prior 33.477s sample.
asi.rollback_reason: No new code change was introduced for this confirmation run; metric was slower than the current best sample, so no additional keep beyond the already-kept fcfa344 candidate.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the 25-frame candidate as stable enough; next inspect whether a readiness predicate can replace fixed waits rather than pushing below 25 blindly.

description: 20-frame initial title settle reproduces unsafe modal/no-character world screenshot

asi.hypothesis: 20 frames may be enough to avoid the 15-frame modal race while recovering some of the 25-frame candidate's margin
asi.rollback_reason: Full oracle failed: screenshot shows 'Connection to Steam interrupted. Returning to title menu.' with no visible character, matching the user's concern about the unsafe 15-frame screenshot. Structured player/save fields alone are insufficient here.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not test lower fixed initial settles. Keep 25 frames as the minimum fixed threshold unless a real readiness predicate can prove the Steam/title modal cannot still be pending.

description: 150-frame menu-build modal grace is unsafe and leaves return-to-title modal over world

asi.hypothesis: The 180-frame PHASE_MENU_BUILD grace may be over-conservative; reducing it to 150 should open the menu earlier while the dialog is already in Loop.
asi.rollback_reason: Full oracle failed: screenshot again shows 'Connection to Steam interrupted. Returning to title menu.' with no visible character. Earlier menu open can hit the same pending Steam/title-modal hazard even with 25-frame initial settle.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep OWN_STEPPER_MODAL_GRACE at 180 unless a positive readiness predicate can prove the Steam/title modal hazard is gone; do not reduce it as a fixed wait.

description: 150-frame modal grace plus captured-dialog-close predicate still leaves return-to-title modal

asi.hypothesis: Waiting for the captured startup MessageBoxDialog to close before load_activate may make the 150-frame modal grace safe while preserving most of its time savings.
asi.rollback_reason: Full oracle failed: screenshot still shows 'Connection to Steam interrupted. Returning to title menu.' The predicate delayed activation until the captured dialog closed, but T_playgame still occurred at frame 269 versus safe 25/180's frame 272, so dialog-close alone is not a sufficient positive readiness predicate.
asi.next_action_hint: If pursuing this path, require an additional post-modal-close settle or a stronger title/Steam modal quiescence signal; otherwise keep fixed modal grace at 180.

description: Semantic title/modal readiness predicate candidate failed before autoload

asi.hypothesis: Replace initial fixed settle with native title-owner/TitleTopDialog Loop readiness and replace modal grace with MessageBoxDialog parent MenuWindowJob result consumption before SetState5.
asi.rollback_reason: Candidate failed early: fast_load_seconds=999, north_star_score=200, autoload_success=0, no player/world. The initial predicate likely waited for a pre-BeginLogo TitleTopDialog Loop state that is not present on this product path, so it never drove the load.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect autoload-debug for the failed artifact, then refine initial readiness. Do not reintroduce a fixed frame count as product; likely need a different native owner/session boundary or a diagnostic hook on BeginLogo/TitleTopDialog state transitions.

description: Modal parent-result quiescence candidate failed before autoload

asi.hypothesis: Keep the known initial 25-frame diagnostic window, remove PHASE_MENU_BUILD modal grace, and wait before SetState5 for the captured MessageBoxDialog parent MenuWindowJob to consume its +0x130 result.
asi.rollback_reason: Candidate again failed before autoload: fast_load_seconds=999, north_star_score=200, title_bootstrap_seen=0, autoload_attempts=0. Removing the grace was not reached; the runtime is failing during early black-screen/title bootstrap under this edited binary.
asi.next_action_hint: Before more product mutation, run a control baseline on reverted code to separate runtime/environment flake from early hook/codegen regression. If baseline passes, reintroduce modal parent tracking as diagnostic-only logging first, not as a gate.

description: Control rerun of reverted settle25 path failed before title bootstrap

asi.hypothesis: Control rerun of the reverted known-good settle25 path should separate runtime/environment failure from the semantic-readiness code changes.
asi.rollback_reason: Control failed with the same early black-screen/title-bootstrap signature: fast_load_seconds=999, north_star_score=200, title_bootstrap_seen=0, autoload_attempts=0. This points to runtime/environment/degradation or harness state, not the readiness predicate edit.
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate runtime degradation/stale launch state and current build/harness before further semantic experiments. Do not draw product conclusions from the last two candidate failures.

description: Control rerun restored full oracle but harness still lacks exact known-good LazyLoader CHAINLOAD payload

asi.hypothesis: restore the runtime baseline before resuming readiness predicate experiments
asi.rollback_reason: User rejected this as not good: although the oracle passed, this did not prove the exact known-good LazyLoader CHAINLOAD harness because runtime_probe.sh currently ignores the CHAINLOAD env toggles and may install er_effects_rs.dll through LOADORDER.
asi.next_action_hint: fix harness/payload setup first: make RUNTIME_LAZYLOAD_CHAINLOAD_DLL=1 produce lazyLoad.ini with [CHAINLOAD] dll=er_effects_rs.dll and no LOADORDER er_effects entry, then rerun the control.
